original_hypothesis.title: Admin pause/blacklist bypass via payGaslessBasefee(): trusted forwarder can move tokens even when paused or payer/paymaster blacklisted
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Token.payGaslessBasefee() calls _update(payer,paymaster,_gaslessBasefee) without adminSanity() checks. If a trusted forwarder is malicious/compromised, it can repeatedly call payGaslessBasefee to drain any payer to any paymaster even while paused and regardless of blacklist status, limited only by payer balance and configured _gaslessBasefee.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While setting trustedForwarder is admin-controlled, compromise/misconfiguration is a realistic operational risk. The on-chain effect is direct balance movement outside the intended transfer compliance controls.
deployment_assumptions: trustedForwarder is set to a contract that can be abused (malicious, compromised, or has its own replay/validation bugs)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Token.sol payGaslessBasefee only checks isTrustedForwarder(msg.sender); no pause/blacklist checks exist in this function.

proof_of_signal.pattern: payGaslessBasefee: if (!isTrustedForwarder(msg.sender)) revert; then _update(payer,paymaster,_gaslessBasefee) with no adminSanity/paused/blacklist checks

preconditions: trustedForwarder is set to attacker-controlled or exploitable contract

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: privileged-only gate (trusted forwarder) but bypasses compliance checks
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: token movement continues under pause/blacklist via basefee path
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no paused() / blacklist enforcement in payGaslessBasefee

analysis_questions: Is payGaslessBasefee intended to be callable only after a successful forwarded transfer, or can it be called standalone? (it can)
Should adminSanity be applied to payer/paymaster in basefee payment?
Can a malicious forwarder call payGaslessBasefee in a loop to drain users?
validation_requirements: Write PoC forwarder that is set as trustedForwarder, then drains victim via repeated payGaslessBasefee
Test behavior under paused() and with blacklisted payer/paymaster
Decide intended policy: block when paused and/or if payer/paymaster blacklisted

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: After becoming trustedForwarder (via admin mistake or compromise), attacker calls payGaslessBasefee(victim, attacker) repeatedly until victim balance depleted, bypassing pause and blacklist.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Targeted draining of user balances in increments of _gaslessBasefee; could affect many users
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires admin clawback or off-chain remediation; on-chain irreversible otherwise

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No tests asserting pause/blacklist constraints on payGaslessBasefee

technical_investigation_focus.gas_analysis_needed: loop feasibility and per-call gas cost
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: monitor GaslessBasefeePaid events for anomalous frequency

validation_result.impact_summary: payGaslessBasefee() bypasses pause and blacklist enforcement, allowing a compromised/malicious trusted forwarder to drain any payer's balance in increments of _gaslessBasefee, even when the contract is paused or parties are blacklisted.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The hypothesis correctly identifies that Token.sol's payGaslessBasefee() function performs a direct _update() call to move tokens without invoking adminSanity(), which is the function responsible for enforcing pause and blacklist compliance in all other transfer paths. Every other token movement function (transfer, transferFrom, transferWithAuthorization, forceTransfer) routes through adminSanity checks. payGaslessBasefee only verifies that msg.sender is the trusted forwarder and that the payer has sufficient balance. This is a genuine code defect—a missing check—regardless of whether exploitation requires a compromised forwarder.

The defect is twofold: (1) a blacklisted payer can still have tokens moved out via this path, and (2) a blacklisted paymaster can receive tokens via this path. Both violate the intended compliance model where blacklisted addresses cannot send or receive tokens.

While the normal Forwarder.execute() flow would fail if the underlying transfer() call fails (due to pause/blacklist), the payGaslessBasefee function is a standalone external function. Any contract set as trustedForwarder can call it directly with arbitrary payer/paymaster parameters without first executing a transfer.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Token.sol lines 116-123 confirm the exact pattern described in the hypothesis. The function checks only isTrustedForwarder(msg.sender) and balance, then calls _update directly. Compare with transferSanity (line 126-129) which calls adminSanity before any _update. ERC20AdminUpgradeable.sol adminSanity (lines 67-73) enforces pause and blacklist. payGaslessBasefee completely skips these checks.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Calls _update() to move tokens from payer to paymaster without checking paused() state or blacklist status of either payer or paymaster. All other transfer paths enforce these checks via adminSanity().
This is the correct pattern showing that all normal transfer paths call adminSanity first. payGaslessBasefee does not follow this pattern.
These are the compliance checks that payGaslessBasefee bypasses entirely—pause enforcement, sender blacklist, recipient blacklist, and self-transfer prevention.

calling_sequence: 1. Attacker's malicious contract (set as trustedForwarder by compromised/mistaken admin) calls payGaslessBasefee(victim, attacker)
2. isTrustedForwarder(msg.sender) check passes because attacker's contract IS the trusted forwarder
3. Balance check: balanceOf(victim) >= _gaslessBasefee passes if victim has tokens
4. _update(victim, attacker, _gaslessBasefee) directly moves tokens - no adminSanity, no pause check, no blacklist check
5. Repeat in a loop to drain victim's full balance in increments of _gaslessBasefee
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The vulnerable path is Token.sol:116-123. When payGaslessBasefee is called by a trusted forwarder contract, tokens are moved via _update() at line 120 without any of the compliance checks that protect all other transfer paths. The adminSanity() function (ERC20AdminUpgradeable.sol:67-73) that enforces pause and blacklist is never invoked. This means: (a) tokens move when paused() == true, (b) tokens move from blacklisted payers, (c) tokens move to blacklisted paymasters, and (d) tokens can even be sent to address(this) since the TransferToContractError check is also skipped.

deployment_assumptions_checked: trustedForwarder is set to a contract that can be abused (malicious, compromised, or has its own replay/validation bugs)
config_validation.deployment_evidence: deployToken.js does not set trustedForwarder or _gaslessBasefee. These are ADMIN-configured post-deployment via setTrustedForwarder() (Token.sol:98) and setGaslessBasefee() (Token.sol:113). The Forwarder.sol contract exists in scope and is intended to be set as trustedForwarder, but there is no deployment script showing this configuration step.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The attack scenario is valid: if ADMIN sets a malicious/compromised address as trustedForwarder (via setTrustedForwarder), that contract can call payGaslessBasefee(victim, attacker) in a loop. Each call drains _gaslessBasefee tokens from victim to attacker. This bypasses pause (contract could be paused for emergency but draining continues) and blacklist (victim or attacker could be blacklisted but transfers proceed). The Forwarder.sol itself could be the attack vector if compromised, since it calls payGaslessBasefee in its execute() function with msg.sender-supplied parameters. Even the legitimate Forwarder has a cross-domain replay vulnerability (INV-ACCESS-003) that could be exploited to trigger unauthorized payGaslessBasefee calls.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH impact. The missing compliance checks represent a code defect that violates the security model. (1) Pause bypass: emergency halt mechanism is ineffective for this transfer path. (2) Blacklist bypass: sanctioned addresses can send/receive tokens through this path, breaking regulatory compliance for a stablecoin. (3) Draining: repeated calls can drain any user's balance at the rate of _gaslessBasefee per transaction. The severity is amplified because this is a regulated EUR stablecoin where compliance controls (pause, blacklist) are critical features.
recommended_mitigations: Add adminSanity(payer, paymaster) check at the beginning of payGaslessBasefee(), or at minimum add: require(!paused()); require(!isBlacklisted(payer)); require(!isBlacklisted(paymaster));
Consider adding require(paymaster != address(this)) to prevent tokens being sent to the contract address
Add rate limiting or per-transaction binding to prevent the trusted forwarder from calling payGaslessBasefee independently of a successful forwarded transfer
Consider making payGaslessBasefee callable only from within execute() by using a transient storage flag or callback pattern to bind basefee payment to an actual forwarded transfer
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy EURFToken via proxy. 2. Admin sets _gaslessBasefee to a non-zero value (e.g., 1000). 3. Deploy a malicious contract that calls payGaslessBasefee in a loop. 4. Admin sets malicious contract as trustedForwarder. 5. Mint tokens to a victim address. 6. Admin pauses contract and/or blacklists victim. 7. Call malicious contract to invoke payGaslessBasefee(victim, attacker) repeatedly. 8. Verify tokens are transferred despite pause/blacklist state.
uncertainties: The actual trusted forwarder (Forwarder.sol) binds payGaslessBasefee calls to successful transfer executions, so under normal operation this path requires a valid meta-tx signature first. The vulnerability is primarily exploitable via a compromised or alternative trusted forwarder.
The impact depends on the configured _gaslessBasefee value—if set to 0, no tokens move. However, a non-zero basefee is required for the gasless relay economic model to function.
additional_testing_needed: Test payGaslessBasefee behavior when contract is paused—verify tokens still transfer
Test payGaslessBasefee with blacklisted payer—verify tokens still transfer from blacklisted address
Test payGaslessBasefee with blacklisted paymaster—verify blacklisted address receives tokens
Test loop draining via custom forwarder contract to measure gas costs and feasibility
Test that payGaslessBasefee can send tokens to address(this) (the token contract itself)
